title: Connect the Anydocs MCP Server to Codex
description: Give Codex a safe way to read and write Anydocs projects.

render.markdown:
# Connect the Anydocs MCP Server to Codex

A practical setup looks like this:

1. Register `@anydocs/mcp` in Codex as a `stdio` MCP server
2. Make sure Codex reads the project's minimal `AGENTS.md`
3. Let Codex treat `anydocs://authoring/guidance` as the detailed workflow source

A good default workflow is:

- `project_open`
- inspect `project_open.authoring.templates`, `resources`, and `resourceTemplates`
- if a page uses `template` / `metadata`, inspect that template's `metadataSchema`
- read `anydocs://authoring/guidance` when the task needs workflow detail or canonical examples
- `page_list` / `page_find` / `page_get`
- `page_create_from_template` or `page_create`
- `page_update` / `page_set_status`
- `nav_get` / `nav_insert` / `nav_delete` / `nav_move`

This keeps page mutations inside Anydocs domain validation, preserves publish rules, and makes agent behavior much more predictable.
render.plainText: Connect the Anydocs MCP server to Codex by registering @anydocs/mcp as a stdio server, letting Codex read the project's minimal AGENTS.md, and using anydocs://authoring/guidance as the detailed workflow source. Inspect template metadataSchema before writing template metadata, then use the page and nav MCP tools for authoring.
